description: Move tasks/ directory under .task-mgr/tasks/ so all task artifacts live alongside the database

criteria: Rust: No warnings from cargo check or cargo clippy
Rust: All tests pass with cargo test
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless required

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works
3. CORRECTNESS - Compiles, type-checks, all tests pass
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Each task delivers working code + tests together
Test boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: resolve_paths() places progress file in same dir as PRD (prd_absolute.parent())
Positive: ensure_directories() creates db_dir/tasks/ and db_dir/tasks/archive/
Unit test: resolve_paths with PRD at .task-mgr/tasks/prd.json places progress at .task-mgr/tasks/progress.txt
Unit test: resolve_paths with prefix 'P1' places progress at .task-mgr/tasks/progress-P1.txt
Unit test: ensure_directories(db_dir) creates db_dir/tasks/ and db_dir/tasks/archive/
Negative: ensure_directories must NOT create a tasks/ dir at project root
Known-bad: if progress_file still uses project_dir.join("tasks"), test with PRD in a different dir would show progress file in wrong location
userStories.description:
Update resolve_paths() and ensure_directories() in src/loop_engine/env.rs to use the DB directory (.task-mgr) as the base for task paths instead of project root.

DO:
- Change resolve_paths() progress file derivation from project_dir.join("tasks") to prd_absolute.parent().unwrap_or(project_dir) — derive from PRD location
- Change ensure_directories() parameter from project_dir to db_dir, use db_dir.join("tasks") internally
- Update the doc comments to reflect the new semantics
- Update all env.rs unit tests that assert progress file paths or tasks dir paths

DO NOT:
- Change the resolve_paths() function signature (no new parameters needed)
- Break the tasks_dir derivation at line 374 (prd_absolute.parent()) — it already works correctly
- Touch test cases that use tmp.path().join("tasks") — those are temp dir structure and stay as-is

Edge cases:
- When prd_absolute.parent() is None (shouldn't happen with absolute paths, but handle gracefully)
- When db_dir doesn't exist yet (ensure_directories should create it)

Known-bad to guard against:
- If progress file is still derived from project_dir.join("tasks"), it would write to ./tasks/ instead of .task-mgr/tasks/, creating orphaned progress files
userStories.notes: Key functions: resolve_paths (env.rs:303), ensure_directories (env.rs:393). The ResolvedPaths struct (env.rs:282) has tasks_dir which is already derived from prd_absolute.parent() — no change needed there. Only the progress_file derivation at line 371 and ensure_directories body at line 394 need changing.
userStories.title: Core path resolution changes in env.rs

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: engine.rs passes db_dir to ensure_directories()
Positive: batch.rs uses dir.join("tasks") for signal file checking
Positive: Existing batch tests still pass
Negative: ensure_directories must NOT receive source_root anymore
Known-bad: if batch.rs still uses project_root.join("tasks"), .stop files would be checked in wrong directory
userStories.description:
Update the callers of ensure_directories() and the tasks_dir computation in batch.rs to use db_dir.

DO:
- engine.rs:705: Change ensure_directories(&run_config.source_root) to ensure_directories(&run_config.db_dir)
- batch.rs:241: Change project_root.join("tasks") to dir.join("tasks") (dir parameter is already the db_dir)
- Update any tests in these files that assert on tasks_dir paths

DO NOT:
- Change the resolve_paths() call in engine.rs:685 — it still receives source_root for PRD resolution, and PRD paths will be .task-mgr/tasks/prd.json which resolves correctly via project_dir.join(prd_file)
- Add new parameters to existing function signatures

Edge cases:
- batch.rs uses tasks_dir for .stop signal file checking — ensure signal files are found at new location
userStories.notes: engine.rs LoopRunConfig has both source_root and db_dir fields. batch.rs run_batch() already receives dir parameter (db_dir) alongside project_root. The dir parameter is what we want for tasks_dir.
userStories.title: Update callers in engine.rs and batch.rs

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: All cli/tests.rs tests pass with new .task-mgr/tasks/ paths
Positive: All display.rs tests pass with new paths
Positive: All init/tests.rs tests pass with updated DB paths
Negative: No remaining hardcoded "tasks/" prefix in test CLI arguments (should be .task-mgr/tasks/)
Unit test: cargo test --lib passes for all updated test files
userStories.description:
Update hardcoded test paths in cli/tests.rs and display.rs that reference tasks/ prefix to use .task-mgr/tasks/ prefix.

DO:
- cli/tests.rs: Update ~24 instances of "tasks/..." in Cli::parse_from() calls and ~9 assertion PathBuf::from("tasks/...") to use ".task-mgr/tasks/..."
- display.rs: Update ~7 instances of "tasks/prd.json" in print_session_banner() and format_session_banner() test calls
- init/tests.rs: Update ~5 prd_files DB path test values if they use "tasks/" prefix

DO NOT:
- Change non-test code in these files
- Change test paths that use tmp.path().join("tasks") — those are temp dir relative
- Change LIKE patterns that use '%prd.json' — those are already portable

Edge cases:
- Some tests may use "tasks/" as both input and expected output — update both consistently
userStories.notes: This is primarily a find-and-replace task. In cli/tests.rs, search for Cli::parse_from calls with "tasks/" and PathBuf::from("tasks/"). In display.rs, search for "tasks/prd.json". In init/tests.rs, search for insert_prd_file and SELECT queries with "tasks/".
userStories.title: Update CLI and display test paths

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: .task-mgr/tasks/ directory is NOT gitignored (negation works)
Positive: .task-mgr/tasks.db IS still gitignored (*.db rule)
Positive: .task-mgr/tasks/progress*.txt IS gitignored
Positive: .task-mgr/loop.lock and other runtime files ARE gitignored
Positive: CLAUDE.md reflects new .task-mgr/tasks/ paths
Positive: All 3 command docs updated consistently
Negative: No remaining references to bare 'tasks/' paths in docs (should be .task-mgr/tasks/)
userStories.description:
Update .gitignore to allow .task-mgr/tasks/ through negation, update CLAUDE.md and all command reference docs.

DO:
- .gitignore: Change '.task-mgr/' to '.task-mgr/*', add '!.task-mgr/tasks/', add '.task-mgr/tasks/progress*.txt'
- Remove old 'tasks/progress*.txt' gitignore line
- CLAUDE.md: Update task file path references (lines 14-16)
- .claude/commands/prd.md: Update all tasks/ references to .task-mgr/tasks/
- .claude/commands/plan-tasks.md: Update all tasks/ references to .task-mgr/tasks/
- .claude/commands/tasks.md: Update all tasks/ references to .task-mgr/tasks/
- scripts/rebuild-from-json.sh: Update example paths

DO NOT:
- Change the .task-mgr/* pattern to anything else — the glob form is required for negation to work
- Remove the *.db gitignore rule — it's still needed for tasks.db

Edge cases:
- .gitignore negation only works with glob (*) not directory trailing slash (/)
userStories.notes: The .gitignore negation pattern must use .task-mgr/* (glob) instead of .task-mgr/ (directory) for negation to work. Git ignores everything in a directory-ignored path and won't check negations. With glob, individual items are ignored but negations can override.
userStories.title: Update gitignore, CLAUDE.md, and command docs

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: All files from ./tasks/ now exist under .task-mgr/tasks/
Positive: .task-mgr/tasks/archive/ has both old archive content and new dated subdirs
Positive: ./tasks/ directory no longer exists
Positive: git status shows clean tracking (old files removed, new files added per gitignore rules)
Negative: No files lost during migration — verify count before and after
Negative: tasks.db must NOT be under .task-mgr/tasks/ — stays at .task-mgr/tasks.db
userStories.description:
Move all files from ./tasks/ to .task-mgr/tasks/, merging with existing content in .task-mgr/tasks/archive/.

DO:
- Move all files from ./tasks/ to .task-mgr/tasks/ (JSON, prompts, PRDs, scripts, learnings, backlog, etc.)
- Merge ./tasks/archive/ contents with existing .task-mgr/tasks/archive/ (the recent archive run already put dated subdirs there)
- git rm the old ./tasks/ tracked files
- git add the new .task-mgr/tasks/ files
- Remove the empty ./tasks/ directory

DO NOT:
- Overwrite files in .task-mgr/tasks/archive/ that already exist from the archive run
- Move tasks.db (it stays at .task-mgr/tasks.db, not .task-mgr/tasks/tasks.db)
- Delete any files — only move them

Edge cases:
- Some files exist in both ./tasks/ and .task-mgr/tasks/archive/ — the archive copies are newer and should be kept
- progress*.txt files should be moved but will be gitignored at the new location
userStories.notes: The .task-mgr/tasks/archive/ directory already has dated subdirs from the recent archive run (2026-03-04-*). The ./tasks/archive/ has older flat files. Use rsync or cp -n to merge without overwriting. Check file counts before and after.
userStories.title: Physical file migration

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: No production code uses project_root.join("tasks") or source_root.join("tasks")
Positive: All tasks_dir computations use db_dir (or equivalent .task-mgr path)
Positive: cargo test passes
Positive: cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.description:
Scan entire codebase for any remaining project_dir.join("tasks") or project_root.join("tasks") patterns that should now use db_dir.join("tasks").

DO:
- Grep for .join("tasks") across all non-test Rust code
- Verify each occurrence uses the correct base path (db_dir, not project root)
- Check status_queries.rs read_deadline_info() — it uses dir.join("tasks") which should already be correct (dir = .task-mgr)
- Verify archive.rs and init/mod.rs still work correctly (they use dir.join("tasks") which is correct)

DO NOT:
- Change code in test helpers that build temp dir structures with .join("tasks")
- Introduce new abstractions — just ensure consistency
userStories.notes: Key files to verify: env.rs, engine.rs, batch.rs, archive.rs, init/mod.rs, status_queries.rs. The archive and init already use dir.join("tasks") where dir = .task-mgr — those are correct.
userStories.title: Review for DRY and consistency

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: All new code reachable from production entry points
Positive: All errors propagated with context (map_err, not unwrap)
Positive: cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check all clean
Positive: .gitignore negation verified — git check-ignore .task-mgr/tasks/test.json returns non-ignored
Negative: No unwrap() in production code paths
Negative: No dead code warnings for new code
Negative: No remaining project_dir.join("tasks") in production code
If issues found: FIX-xxx tasks added to JSON with priority 50-98
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring. Run full test suite.

DO: Check every changed function for error handling, check callers still work, verify no dead code.
DO NOT: Skip the integration wiring check — unconnected code is the #1 failure mode.
userStories.notes: If issues found: add FIX-xxx tasks to JSON, commit JSON changes. Loop picks them up automatically. If no issues: mark passes:true with note 'Clean review'.
userStories.title: Code review + final verification
